title: BTS Concert Surprise Affects Company's Money
summary: BTS, a famous K-pop band, had a big free concert in Seoul, South Korea. All seven members performed together for the first time since 2022. Fewer fans came than expected—only about 104,000 instead of 250,000. This made shares in Hybe, the company that manages BTS, drop by 15.5%. The concert was also streamed on Netflix to many countries, which might have affected how many people attended in person. BTS released a new album called Arirang, which sold nearly 4 million copies on its first day. Hybe relies on BTS for money, and the band faces competition from other groups. The concert started their world tour with 82 dates.

the company that manages BTS and makes money from them
a live music performance for fans to watch
parts of a company that people can buy and sell, affecting its value

a collection of songs released together, like Arirang

What happened to Hybe's shares after the concert?
Where was the BTS concert held?
What is the name of BTS's new album?
How was the concert shown to people in other countries?
Why did BTS not perform together for a while before this concert?

background_read: BTS is a very popular K-pop band from South Korea with seven members: Jin, Suga, J-Hope, RM, Jimin, V, and Jung Kook. They took a break starting in 2022 because the members had to do mandatory military service, which is required for men in South Korea. During this time, their company Hybe made less money. K-pop is a type of music from Korea that has fans all over the world. Companies like Hybe earn money from album sales, concerts, and streaming. When a big event like a concert doesn't go as planned, it can affect the company's value, measured by shares that people trade.
Article_Structure: WHO: BTS, Hybe company, and fans
WHAT: A concert with lower attendance than expected, causing Hybe's shares to drop
WHERE: Gwanghwamun Square in Seoul, South Korea
WHY: Because fewer fans came in person, possibly due to streaming on Netflix and military service break

perspectives.description: The low concert turnout hurt Hybe's financial value, showing how dependent the company is on BTS.
